APIConfHasNoGoodsMovements.title: Confirmation: No Goods Movement via API

BreakDurationUnitISOCode.title: ISO code for measurement units. An ISO code can be assigned to several internal measurement units of a dimension.
BreakDurationUnitISOCode.description: The ISO code is important for EDI. It is used to convert the internal SAP measurement units into standard measurement units. Data exchange via EDI requires internal measurement units to be converted into standard measurement units.A list of the current internationally agreed ISO codes can be ordered from the UN commission responsible (WP.4 Trade Facilitation Recommendations, Recommendation 20):UN Economic Commission for EuropeInformation OfficePalais des Nations1211 GenevaSwitzerlande-mail: info.ece@unece.orgor viewed directly on the Internet:http://www.unece.org/cefact/codesfortrade/codes_index.htmFor more information, see SAP Note 69063.

OpenReservationsIsCleared.title: Indicator specifying that with a final confirmation, the indicator "delivery completed" is set for all open reservations that are assigned to the confirmed operation.
OpenReservationsIsCleared.description: The indicator is only set for the open reservations of the components that are not to be backflushed and for batch summary records for the components to be backflushed.The operation is given the system status "finally delivered" as well as the system status "finally confirmed".

Batch.title: Assigns a material that is manufactured in batches or production lots to a specific batch.

Customer.title: Uniquely identifies a customer.
Customer.description: This customer number is used for managing customer special stocks.

EntryUnit.title: Specifies the unit of measure in which the goods movement or inventory count is entered.
EntryUnit.description: Enter a unit of measure here only if it differs from the default unit of measure.There are the following default units of measure:the order unit for goods receipts with reference to purchase ordersthe production unit for goods receipts with reference to production ordersthe unit of issue for other goods movementsIf the default unit of measure has not been defined in the material master record, the system will suggest the stockkeeping unit.

EntryUnitISOCode.title: ISO code for measurement units. An ISO code can be assigned to several internal measurement units of a dimension.
EntryUnitISOCode.description: The ISO code is important for EDI. It is used to convert the internal SAP measurement units into standard measurement units. Data exchange via EDI requires internal measurement units to be converted into standard measurement units.A list of the current internationally agreed ISO codes can be ordered from the UN commission responsible (WP.4 Trade Facilitation Recommendations, Recommendation 20):UN Economic Commission for EuropeInformation OfficePalais des Nations1211 GenevaSwitzerlande-mail: info.ece@unece.orgor viewed directly on the Internet:http://www.unece.org/cefact/codesfortrade/codes_index.htmFor more information, see SAP Note 69063.

EntryUnitSAPCode.title: Unit of Measurement, Internal SAP Code (No Conversion)

IsCompletelyDelivered.title: Indicates that the item is to be regarded as closed.

Material.title: Number of the material that is to be produced with this order.

MaterialDocument.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.
MaterialDocument.description: Number of the material document with which a goods movement was posted.Together with the material document year, the document number constitutes the key via which a material document is accessed.

MaterialDocumentItem.title: Specifies the number that uniquely identifies an item in .

MaterialDocumentItemText.title: Contains an explanatory text on the line item.
MaterialDocumentItemText.description: Line item texts can be used internally and externally. To be able to distinguish between these, you must begin texts for external use with "*". These texts can then be printed on all correspondence, dunning notices, payment advice notes, etc. The asterisk is removed when the text is printed.This function is only available in the FI component. It cannot be used in Materials Management.

MaterialDocumentYear.title: Calendar year in which the material document was posted.
MaterialDocumentYear.description: In conjunction with the document number, the material document year constitutes the key via which a material document is accessed.

ProductionSupplyArea.title: The production supply area (PSA) serves as a place for interim storage on the shop floor and is used to make material directly available for production purposes.
ProductionSupplyArea.description: The PSA is used in Kanban and JIT Outbound processing and in Extended Warehouse Management.

Reservation.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.

ReservationItem.title: Item Number of Reservation / Dependent Requirements

SalesOrder.title: Number of the sales order to which the order is to be settled.

SalesOrderItem.title: Item in the sales order to which the order is to be settled.

ShelfLifeExpirationDate.title: The date is set upon goods receipt in one of the following ways:
ShelfLifeExpirationDate.description: You enter it yourself.You enter a date of production, and the system calculates the shelf life expiration date from the date of production plus the shelf life in days (from the material master record).NoteThis date can still be changed upon receipt of the usage decision for an inspection lot.

StorageBin.title: The storage bin (sometimes referred to as a "slot") is the smallest addressable unit in a warehouse. It identifies the exact location in the warehouse where goods can be stored.
StorageBin.description: A storage bin can be further sub-divided into bin sections. Several different materials (quants) can be stored in one bin at the same time.

StorageType.title: The storage type is a subdivision of a complex, physical warehouse. Different storage types are identified by their warehousing technique, form of organization, or their function.
StorageType.description: A typical warehouse could have the following storage types:Goods receipt areaPicking areaHigh-rack storage areaBulk storage areaOpen storage areaGoods issue area

Supplier.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.
Supplier.description: With the supplier number, information from the supplier master record (such as the supplier's address and bank details) is copied into a purchasing document (such as a request for quotation or a purchase order).You can use the supplier number to keep track of requests for quotation, purchase orders and outline agreements.

WBSElementExternalID.title: External 24-character name of an operative WBS element without a conversion routine stored in the domain.

MaterialDocument.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.
MaterialDocument.description: Number of the material document with which a goods movement was posted.Together with the material document year, the document number constitutes the key via which a material document is accessed.

MaterialDocumentItem.title: Specifies the number that uniquely identifies an item in .

OrderOperationInternalID.title: Internal Identifier of an Operation or Activity

Language.title: The language key indicates
Language.description: - the language in which texts are displayed,- the language in which you enter texts,- the language in which the system prints texts.

Material.title: Number of the material that is to be produced with this order.

IsFinalConfirmation.title: Specifies that no further confirmations are required.
IsFinalConfirmation.description: The system assigns the 'finally confirmed' system status.

OpenReservationsIsCleared.title: Indicator specifying that with a final confirmation, the indicator "delivery completed" is set for all open reservations that are assigned to the confirmed operation.
OpenReservationsIsCleared.description: The indicator is only set for the open reservations of the components that are not to be backflushed and for batch summary records for the components to be backflushed.The operation is given the system status "finally delivered" as well as the system status "finally confirmed".

OrderConfirmationRecordType.description: There are confirmations for an operation time event (such as, record type B10 start of processing) or for an operation time ticket (such as, record type L40 finish).

OrderOperationInternalID.title: Internal Identifier of an Operation or Activity

Language.title: The language key indicates
Language.description: - the language in which texts are displayed,- the language in which you enter texts,- the language in which the system prints texts.

Material.title: Number of the material that is to be produced with this order.

IsFinalConfirmation.title: Specifies that no further confirmations are required.
IsFinalConfirmation.description: The system assigns the 'finally confirmed' system status.

OpenReservationsIsCleared.title: Indicator specifying that with a final confirmation, the indicator "delivery completed" is set for all open reservations that are assigned to the confirmed operation.
OpenReservationsIsCleared.description: The indicator is only set for the open reservations of the components that are not to be backflushed and for batch summary records for the components to be backflushed.The operation is given the system status "finally delivered" as well as the system status "finally confirmed".

APIConfHasNoGoodsMovements.title: Confirmation: No Goods Movement via API

OrderConfirmationRecordType.title: Specifies which PP confirmation is to be executed.
OrderConfirmationRecordType.description: There are confirmations for an operation time event (such as, record type B10 start of processing) or for an operation time ticket (such as, record type L40 finish).

LastChangeDate.title: Date on which this rate was last changed.

LastChangedByUser.title: Name of Person who Changed Object

ConfirmationExternalEntryTime.title: Time at which the document was posted and saved in the system.

EnteredByExternalUser.title: Name of the person, who originally entered the confirmation, if it was created in a non-SAP system or using SAP fast entry for confirmations.

ExternalSystemConfirmation.title: External Key for Confirmation (for example, from PDC System)

BusinessProcessConfirmedQty.title: Activity that you want to confirm for this business process.

MaterialDocument.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.
MaterialDocument.description: Number of the material document with which a goods movement was posted.Together with the material document year, the document number constitutes the key via which a material document is accessed.

MaterialDocumentItem.title: Specifies the number that uniquely identifies an item in .

MaterialDocumentYear.title: Calendar year in which the material document was posted.
MaterialDocumentYear.description: In conjunction with the document number, the material document year constitutes the key via which a material document is accessed.

Material.title: Number of the material that is to be produced with this order.

Reservation.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.

ReservationItem.title: Item Number of Reservation / Dependent Requirements

ProductionSupplyArea.title: The production supply area (PSA) serves as a place for interim storage on the shop floor and is used to make material directly available for production purposes.
ProductionSupplyArea.description: The PSA is used in Kanban and JIT Outbound processing and in Extended Warehouse Management.

Batch.title: Assigns a material that is manufactured in batches or production lots to a specific batch.

SalesOrder.title: Number of the sales order to which the order is to be settled.

SalesOrderItem.title: Item in the sales order to which the order is to be settled.

WBSElementExternalID.title: External 24-character name of an operative WBS element without a conversion routine stored in the domain.

Supplier.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.
Supplier.description: With the supplier number, information from the supplier master record (such as the supplier's address and bank details) is copied into a purchasing document (such as a request for quotation or a purchase order).You can use the supplier number to keep track of requests for quotation, purchase orders and outline agreements.

Customer.title: Uniquely identifies a customer.
Customer.description: This customer number is used for managing customer special stocks.

IsCompletelyDelivered.title: Indicates that the item is to be regarded as closed.

ShelfLifeExpirationDate.title: The date is set upon goods receipt in one of the following ways:
ShelfLifeExpirationDate.description: You enter it yourself.You enter a date of production, and the system calculates the shelf life expiration date from the date of production plus the shelf life in days (from the material master record).NoteThis date can still be changed upon receipt of the usage decision for an inspection lot.

StorageType.title: The storage type is a subdivision of a complex, physical warehouse. Different storage types are identified by their warehousing technique, form of organization, or their function.
StorageType.description: A typical warehouse could have the following storage types:Goods receipt areaPicking areaHigh-rack storage areaBulk storage areaOpen storage areaGoods issue area

StorageBin.title: The storage bin (sometimes referred to as a "slot") is the smallest addressable unit in a warehouse. It identifies the exact location in the warehouse where goods can be stored.
StorageBin.description: A storage bin can be further sub-divided into bin sections. Several different materials (quants) can be stored in one bin at the same time.

MaterialDocumentItemText.title: Contains an explanatory text on the line item.
MaterialDocumentItemText.description: Line item texts can be used internally and externally. To be able to distinguish between these, you must begin texts for external use with "*". These texts can then be printed on all correspondence, dunning notices, payment advice notes, etc. The asterisk is removed when the text is printed.This function is only available in the FI component. It cannot be used in Materials Management.

EntryUnit.title: Specifies the unit of measure in which the goods movement or inventory count is entered.
EntryUnit.description: Enter a unit of measure here only if it differs from the default unit of measure.There are the following default units of measure:the order unit for goods receipts with reference to purchase ordersthe production unit for goods receipts with reference to production ordersthe unit of issue for other goods movementsIf the default unit of measure has not been defined in the material master record, the system will suggest the stockkeeping unit.

EntryUnitISOCode.title: ISO code for measurement units. An ISO code can be assigned to several internal measurement units of a dimension.
EntryUnitISOCode.description: The ISO code is important for EDI. It is used to convert the internal SAP measurement units into standard measurement units. Data exchange via EDI requires internal measurement units to be converted into standard measurement units.A list of the current internationally agreed ISO codes can be ordered from the UN commission responsible (WP.4 Trade Facilitation Recommendations, Recommendation 20):UN Economic Commission for EuropeInformation OfficePalais des Nations1211 GenevaSwitzerlande-mail: info.ece@unece.orgor viewed directly on the Internet:http://www.unece.org/cefact/codesfortrade/codes_index.htmFor more information, see SAP Note 69063.

EntryUnitSAPCode.title: Unit of Measurement, Internal SAP Code (No Conversion)

MaterialDocument.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document.
MaterialDocument.description: Number of the material document with which a goods movement was posted.Together with the material document year, the document number constitutes the key via which a material document is accessed.

MaterialDocumentItem.title: Specifies the number that uniquely identifies an item in .

MaterialDocumentYear.title: Calendar year in which the material document was posted.
MaterialDocumentYear.description: In conjunction with the document number, the material document year constitutes the key via which a material document is accessed.

Material.title: Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the material.

Batch.title: Assigns a material that is manufactured in batches or production lots to a specific batch.

Characteristic.title: Name that uniquely identifies a characteristic.

CharcValue.title: Value of a characteristic.
CharcValue.description: If the characteristic has character format (CHAR), this field contains a single character string.Values in time format (TIME) must have the format HH:MM:SS. The separators are set automatically. Example:  112855  --> 11:28:55The values in date format (DATE) must have the format DD.MM.YYYY. The separators are set automatically. Example: 110100  --> 11.01.2000NoteIf you valuate validity parameters, you cannot specify intervals. You can only specify individual values.The following description does not apply to the valuation of validity parameters:If the characteristic format is numeric (NUM), time (TIME), or date (DATE), you can enter a number or (if allowed) an interval.Intervals are written in the following format:Value1 - Value2 (from Value1 to Value2)>Value1 (greater than Value1)>=Value1 (greater than or equal to Value1)<Value2 (less than Value2)<=Value2 (less than or equal to Value2)>Value1 - <Value2 (greater than Value1 and less than Value2)Value1 - <Value2 (greater than or equal to Value1 and less thanValue2)>Value1 - Value2 (greater than Value1 and less than or equal toValue2)If the characteristic has more than one value, you can enter multiple values separated by semicolons.If you have maintained a unit of measure for numeric values in the formatting data of the characteristic, the unit is displayed automatically when you confirm.NoteIf you want to define intervals as allowed values of a characteristic, you must define the characteristic as "multiple-value" when you maintain the characteristic in the Classification System. This also allows you to enter more than one interval.However, if you assign more than one interval to a characteristic of a class during configuration (for example, 1-2 and 5-6 for characteristic LENGTH), the system only reads one of the intervals when generating an inspection lot in QM. To avoid this problem, do NOT assign more than one interval to a characteristic.

count.description: The number of entities in the collection. Available when using the $inlinecount query option, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/).

innererror.description: The structure of this object is service-specific

top.name: $top
top.description: Show only the first n items, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/)

skip.name: $skip
skip.description: Skip the first n items, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/)

count.name: $inlinecount
count.description: Include count of items, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/)

apply.name: $apply
apply.description: Apply basic grouping and aggregation functionality, see [Aggregation](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata-data-aggregation-ext/v4.0/odata-data-aggregation-ext-v4.0.html)
